paper_title: Modeling of Vertical Land Movement in Holocene Peat and Clay Layers

methods.description: A 1-dimensional mathematical model was developed to quantify vertical land movement in Holocene peat and clay layers due to groundwater flow and deformation. The model incorporates an isotache-based, viscoelastic compression model to calculate deformation, focusing on creep as the primary cause of irreversible deformation.

steps.description: Build a 1-dimensional mathematical model for Holocene peat and clay layers.
steps.searchable_summary: develop a 1D mathematical model for land movement in peat and clay layers
inputs: Holocene peat and clay layers data

steps.description: Calculate coupled groundwater flow and deformation using an isotache-based, viscoelastic compression model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate groundwater flow and deformation using isotache-based model

methods.description: The model was executed for a three-layer configuration to simulate the impacts of different water table scenarios on vertical land movement over a 25-year period.

steps.description: Run the model for a three-layer configuration with specified thicknesses for anthropogenic, peat, and clay layers.

steps.description: Simulate seasonal water table variations using a sine-function.

steps.description: Calculate vertical land movement and analyze subsidence rates.
